title: Ticket Relationship Support in hlx CLI
description: Adds --after, --reference, and --implement-from flags to tickets create, displays relationship data in tickets get and list, and updates all documentation surfaces.

steps.title: Import resolveTicket utility
steps.description: Added import for resolveTicket from resolve-ticket.js to resolve ticket references (short IDs, internal IDs, numeric refs) to internal IDs for the relationship flags.

steps.title: Updated usage string with new flags
steps.description: The inline help string now includes [--after <ticket-ref>] [--reference <ref1,ref2>] [--implement-from <ticket-ref>] at the end.

steps.title: Relationship flag parsing
steps.description: Three new optional flags parsed using getFlag(): --after (single ticket ref), --reference (comma-separated refs), --implement-from (single ticket ref). Each is resolved via resolveTicket() with try/catch error handling matching the existing --repos pattern.

steps.title: Reference splitting and max-5 validation
steps.description: The --reference flag value is split on commas, trimmed, filtered for empty strings, and validated client-side to contain at most 5 references before resolving each sequentially.

steps.title: POST body with relationship fields
steps.description: The hxFetch POST body now conditionally spreads afterTicketId, implementFromTicketId, and referencedTicketIds only when the corresponding flags were provided and resolved.

steps.title: Server error extraction with em-dash parsing
steps.description: The hxFetch call is wrapped in try/catch. On error, the code splits the error message on the em-dash separator, attempts to JSON.parse the body portion, and extracts the server's error field for clean display. Falls back to the raw message on parse failure.

steps.title: RelatedTicket helper type
steps.description: New shared type for relationship objects returned by the server: { id, title, status, shortId, mode, approvalStatus }. Used by afterTicket, implementFromTicket, and referencedTickets.

steps.title: TicketDetail type extended with relationship fields
steps.description: Six new fields added: afterTicketId, afterTicket, implementFromTicketId, implementFromTicket, referencedTicketIds, referencedTickets. These match the server's GET /api/tickets/:id response shape.

steps.title: Conditional relationship display
steps.description: Three conditional sections added to printTicketDetail(): 'Depends on' (afterTicket), 'Implements' (implementFromTicket), 'References' (referencedTickets). Each only renders when the data is present, preserving identical output for tickets without relationships.

steps.title: TicketItem type extended with relationship fields
steps.description: Four new fields added to match the list endpoint response: afterTicketId, afterTicket (with shortId for display), implementFromTicketId, referencedTicketIds.

steps.title: Dependency indicator in list output
steps.description: New afterTag computed from ticket.afterTicket.shortId and appended to the output line. Only appears for tickets with a dependency, preserving existing alignment.

steps.title: Updated ticketsUsage() with new flags
steps.description: The top-level tickets usage help now includes the three new relationship flags on the create command line.

steps.title: Updated case 'create' help with new flags
steps.description: The create subcommand help string also includes the three new flags.

steps.title: New flag rows in documentation table
steps.description: Three new rows added to the tickets create flags table: --after, --reference, --implement-from with descriptions.

steps.title: Worked examples for ticket relationships
steps.description: Four new worked examples added: creating a dependent ticket, cross-referencing tickets, implementing from research, and viewing relationships.

steps.title: Relationship command examples in skill docs
steps.description: Three new examples in the Ticket Management section showing --after, --reference, and --implement-from usage.

steps.title: Updated Action Commands create reference
steps.description: The tickets create command in the Action Commands section now includes all three new relationship flags.
